name: Azure Machine Configuration hardening packages (win-server control IDs, displayName prefix only)
changes: Control IDs for packages and Excel use prefix 'win-server-' (for example: win-server-ACCT-001).
ControlIdPolicyPrefix in packages/machine-configuration.config.json is used ONLY for Azure Policy displayName (human-readable).
Inserted audit-only control for built-in Administrator name at SECO-003 (no remediation).
Updated scripts and policy templates accordingly; no build outputs are written inside package folders.
Renamed config key ControlIdPrefix -> ControlIdPolicyPrefix and updated scripts/docs accordingly (with fallback).

Enhanced policy templates (deployIfNotExists.enhanced.sample.json) now scope to Windows Server OS images using Microsoft.Compute/imageOffer and Microsoft.Compute/imageSKU (Azure Marketplace URN fields). Baseline deployIfNotExists.json remains unchanged.
Fixed SECO package build scripts to call the correct DSC configuration names; aligned Configuration.ps1 'Configuration <name>' blocks with their win-server-SECO-0xx control IDs.
Corrected build.ps1 / hydrate-policy.ps1 headers so each script references its own package control ID and setting title.
Removed any alternate policy scoping options; enhanced policy remains scoped by Microsoft.Compute/imageOffer and Microsoft.Compute/imageSKU only.
Fixed per-package hydrate-policy.ps1 scripts: corrected ControlId/Config initialization order; removed undefined variables.
Fixed scripts/hydrate-policy-templates.ps1 package discovery to work with win-server-* package folders (discover by hydrate-policy.ps1 presence).

Quality check fixes: corrected SECO package Configuration.ps1 headers/resource IDs and regenerated README.md for win-server-SECO-004..017 to align Control IDs, configuration names, and parameter paths.
Renamed all package folders to use ONLY the ControlId (e.g., packages/win-server-ACCT-001). Removed descriptive suffix from folder names.
Improved policy definition displayName/description text to include setting title and suggested value for human readability.
Updated build/hydrate scripts to embed per-setting metadata (title/path/value) and generate readable policy displayName/description; ControlIdPolicyPrefix is still applied only to policy displayName.
Reverted contentUri/contentHash/contentManagedIdentity to hydrate-time injection using placeholders in policy JSON.
Policies now expose only supported Azure Policy parameters (effect, assignmentType; enhanced also requiredUserAssignedIdentityResourceId). Package URI/hash/UAMI are injected by hydrate scripts.
Fixed build.ps1 in all packages: MOF path detection now matches DSC output (OutputPath\localhost.mof) and falls back to recursive search.
Fixed PowerShell format placeholder in build.ps1 error message (use '{0}' for -f operator).
Added detailed inline comments to all PowerShell scripts (package build/hydrate/configuration and repository scripts) to improve readability and explain each processing step.
Updated all package README.md files: replaced manual remediation with explicit GUI + command-line verification steps to confirm settings were applied.

Fixed Azure Portal policy definition import: hydration and baseline policy generation now emit a *.portal.json variant (properties-only) to avoid nested properties.properties errors in the Portal JSON editor.
Fixed Azure Policy wildcard constraint: replaced imageOffer like '*WS2016*' style patterns with contains 'WS2016' (and WS2019/WS2022/WS2025) in all enhanced policy templates.

Removed invalid VM extension array alias checks (Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/extensions[*]) from all enhanced policy templates; Azure Policy doesn't support that alias. Extension prerequisites should be enforced via the prerequisite initiative/policies.
Quality check v12: fixed policy guestConfiguration.name mismatches in win-server-SECO-004..016 so each policy deploys and evaluates its own package assignment.
Deep static quality check re-run: package count, Excel/package ID parity, JSON parsing, DINE fields, invalid alias/wildcard checks, and policy/package identifier consistency all passed.
No functional source refactor in this variant. Package-local policy JSON sources remain under each package.
